title: frameobject/sys: a running frame's f_trace is written by trace_trampoline without the frame's critical section, racing the @critical_section f_trace_opcodes/f_trace setters
description: trace_trampoline does Py_XSETREF(frame->f_trace, result) on every traced event with no lock, while the Python-level frame.f_trace / frame.f_trace_opcodes accessors are all @critical_section. A second thread setting f_trace_opcodes on a running thread's frame (reachable via sys._current_frames()) reads self->f_trace under the frame lock while the owning thread races it with the unlocked trampoline write; the setter's critical section protects nothing.
sites: Python/sysmodule.c trace_trampoline:1125 (WRITE Py_XSETREF(frame->f_trace, result); no critical section)
Python/legacy_tracing.c sys_trace_instruction_func:305 (sibling face: READ frame->f_trace_opcodes flag on eval hot path)
Objects/frameobject.c frame_trace_set_impl:1881 / frame_trace_opcodes_set_impl:1154,1160 (WRITE side of f_trace / f_trace_opcodes under @critical_section)
race_kind: missing critical section on internal write path (per-frame legacy-trace state: f_trace pointer + f_trace_opcodes/f_trace_lines flags)
shared_state: PyFrameObject.f_trace (PyObject*, per-frame local trace function) and adjacent f_trace_opcodes/f_trace_lines char flags; a running frame shared across threads via sys._current_frames()
severity: low-moderate (real data race on frame->f_trace; latent refcount-corruption / use-after-free if f_trace is written from two threads, since the write path is Py_XSETREF; requires touching another thread's live frame)
repro_reliability: deterministic, ~0.6s, exit 66 (3/3 runs); run 1 hit the exact seeded signature (trace_trampoline write / frame_trace_opcodes_set_impl read), other runs surface sibling faces of the same shared-frame trace-state race
suggested_fix: wrap the f_trace mutations in trace_trampoline (Py_XSETREF at :1125 and Py_CLEAR at :1120) in Py_BEGIN/END_CRITICAL_SECTION(frame), matching the @critical_section frame accessors; use FT_ATOMIC load/store for the f_trace_lines/f_trace_opcodes char flags read on the eval hot path. A relaxed atomic on the f_trace pointer alone is insufficient because it participates in refcounting.
notes: Legacy sys.settrace layer bridged onto PEP 669 (sys.monitoring) instrumentation. The @critical_section decorators on frame_trace_get/set and frame_trace_opcodes_get/set are recent FT hardening; the internal trace trampoline + eval-loop reads (instrumentation.c:1366 f_trace_lines, legacy_tracing.c:190/305 f_trace_opcodes) were not brought under the same discipline. bdb only touches the current thread's own frames, so the debugger itself does not trigger it; the race needs a second thread mutating a running thread's frame. Audit Objects/frameobject.c against the gh-116738 free-threading audit list.

related_issues.note: free-threading thread-safety audit umbrella; the trace trampoline / per-frame f_trace mutation path should be on that list (unverified, no internet).

assessment: Real, NEW, LOW-PRIORITY design-edge: trace_trampoline writes frame->f_trace via Py_XSETREF unlocked while the Python-level f_trace/f_trace_opcodes accessors are @critical_section. Only bites when deliberately mutating another thread's currently-executing frame (via sys._current_frames()); normal debuggers touch own-thread frames only. Refcount-UAF potential but low reach. Umbrella-candidate at most.
